questions: AdminApprovalView click — should the admin's approval also trigger an immediate `safe_delete_messages` cleanup of the wallet-prompt and receipt-confirmation prompts in the thread? The brief says 'delete stale flow messages in the thread via safe_delete_messages' — I'm assuming YES, and that stale = (old prompt_message_id values stored on the intent). Confirm or specify an explicit cleanup policy.

For `PaymentActorKind`: should I add a new `ADMIN_CLICK` kind for auditing admin DM approvals, or reuse an existing kind (e.g., `AUTO` or a manual actor kind)? Adding a new enum value is a ~3-line change in `payment_service.py` but crosses a 'do not modify payment_service' boundary. Confirm the new enum is acceptable.

Batch tool upper bound — the brief uses '16 payments' as an example but doesn't set a hard max. I assumed 1..20. Confirm or set an explicit ceiling (batch size policy matters for admin DM fan-out and rate limiting).

The brief mentions a new `manual_review` terminal-ish state for negative/timeout escalations. This is not explicitly spelled out in the task text but is implied by 'transitions to manual_review' in the test expectations and suggested approach. I'm adding `manual_review` to the CHECK constraint. Confirm this is correct and not a naming collision with existing workflows.

For the admin-only enforcement of `upsert_wallet_for_user`: since identity routing already restricts the agent to admin authors, should the executor still do its own independent `ADMIN_USER_ID` env check, or is trusting the cog-level identity gate sufficient? I'm assuming defense-in-depth (both) — confirm.

success_criteria.criterion: Identity router in on_message has exactly three branches keyed on author.id: admin → agent path, active-intent recipient → state machine, else → return. No content-based dispatch remains on the hot path.

SQL migration extends admin_payment_intents CHECK to include awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation, awaiting_admin_approval, and manual_review; adds ambiguous_reply_count column; is idempotent (re-runnable).
Pending-recipient state machine makes zero Anthropic API calls on any path — verifiable by patching self._classifier_client to a Mock that raises on invocation in tests.

wallet_registry.verified_at is set only via mark_wallet_verified and only after both on-chain test confirmation AND recipient text-ack (positive classification) — no code path sets verified_at based on on-chain confirmation alone.
Admin DM approval gate (AdminApprovalView) fires exactly once per real admin_chat payment, never for is_test=True, never for producer='grants'. A batch of N produces exactly N admin DMs.
AdminApprovalView is persistent (timeout=None) and re-registered on PaymentUICog.cog_load for every admin_payment_intent with status=awaiting_admin_approval — restart recovery works.

send_confirmation_request is NOT invoked for producer='admin_chat' real payments (the call at admin_chat_cog.py:393 is deleted); it IS still invoked for producer='grants' at grants_cog.py:719 (grants flow regression-free).

Auto-skip branch: when wallet_registry.verified_at IS NOT NULL, initiate_payment and initiate_batch_payment create the real payment directly (is_test=False) and enter awaiting_admin_approval — no test payment row is created.

initiate_batch_payment is all-or-none: if any element fails validation, zero intents are persisted; on success, all N intents are created in a single Supabase insert and the per-intent pipeline fans out correctly.

Negative recipient ack and second-ambiguous recipient ack both tag the admin in the source thread with full context (intent_id, test tx signature, recipient wallet) and transition the intent to manual_review.
24h timeout sweep is a background task running at ≥5-minute intervals that correctly transitions stale awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation intents to manual_review and DMs admin.

upsert_wallet_for_user is admin-only (cog-level + executor-level check), validates wallet_address via is_valid_solana_address, never sets verified_at, and surfaces WalletUpdateBlockedError to the admin.

Three new query tools (query_payment_state, query_wallet_state, list_recent_payments) are read-only, apply existing wallet redaction, and reject LLM-injected wallet_address/recipient_wallet params via the pop-and-warn pattern.

All pass_to_pass test suites listed in the brief remain green: test_payment_state_machine, test_payment_cog_split, test_solana_client, test_payment_race, test_payment_reconcile, test_payment_authorization, test_safe_delete_messages, test_check_payment_invariants, test_tx_signature_history.

All fail_to_pass tests listed in the brief pass after implementation (identity router branches, state machine keywords, auto-skip, batch atomicity, upsert admin check, AdminApprovalView persistence + click, admin DM fail-closed, PaymentConfirmView gating, 24h sweep, query tool safety, approved-member no-route).

Hardening invariants preserved: per-user-per-day caps, canonical wallet swap defense, idempotency collision detection, WalletUpdateBlockedError, RLS, prompt-injection hardening (wallet lookups only from wallet_registry), null-recipient fail-closed in PaymentService.confirm_payment.

Cleanup + polish invariants preserved: PaymentActor/PRODUCER_FLOWS registry, tx_signature_history column, PaymentWorkerCog/PaymentUICog split, safe_delete_messages primitive, reconcile_with_chain, dynamic priority fees, /payment-resolve, docs/payments.md, docs/runbook-payments.md, scripts/check_payment_invariants.py, structured tx_confirm_decision logging.
solana_client.py and solana_provider.py are not modified by this change (rebroadcast loop, priority fees, status.err handling untouched).
grants_cog.py is not modified by this change; grants flow still uses PaymentConfirmView end-to-end.

_classify_payment_reply and _check_pending_payment_reply are fully removed once the reconciler stops calling them, or left as dead code with a comment if deletion is deferred — but no live code path invokes the LLM classifier on the recipient state machine.
MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT and _can_user_message_bot are left in place as documented dead code under identity routing (no cleanup in this plan).
Confirmation keyword parser uses hardcoded positive/negative keyword sets with case-insensitive whole-word matching (and emoji substring for 👍). No LLM fallback.
AdminApprovalView custom_id f'payment_admin_approve:{payment_id}' does not collide with any existing persistent view custom_id (verified by grep).

13 manual smoke-test scenarios pass in dev guild: (a) first-time unverified recipient full flow; (b) verified recipient auto-skip flow; (c) batch of 3 mixed verified/unverified; (d) negative ack escalation; (e) ambiguous ack 2x escalation; (f) admin DM click; (g) bot restart mid awaiting_admin_approval and view re-registration; (h) 24h timeout sweep with forged updated_at; (i) upsert_wallet_for_user success + DM; (j) upsert_wallet_for_user during active intent (blocked); (k) grants flow unchanged; (l) query_* tools return correct redacted data; (m) approved non-admin member mention is ignored.

assumptions: Supabase `.insert([list])` is atomic per PostgREST request, so `create_admin_payment_intents_batch` gets true all-or-none semantics without needing a Postgres RPC wrapper. If production behavior differs, we fall back to a SQL RPC function wrapping N inserts in a txn.
`prompt_message_id` is reused (overwritten) for both wallet prompt and receipt-confirmation prompt rather than adding a second column. Only the latest prompt matters for safe-delete.
A new `ambiguous_reply_count integer default 0` column is added to `admin_payment_intents` (no JSONB metadata column exists today to piggyback on).

`manual_review` is added as a new allowed status value in the CHECK constraint (for negative ack + 24h timeout escalation targets). It is non-terminal from the unique-index perspective — active intents in `manual_review` still block new intents for the same recipient until explicitly cancelled via `/payment-resolve` or cog tooling.
`MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT` and `_can_user_message_bot` are left in place as dead code under identity routing. No cleanup commit in this plan.
`_classify_payment_reply` and `_check_pending_payment_reply` are deleted in the same commit as Step 14 once the reconciler no longer calls them. Leaving them in would be dead code.

`PaymentActorKind.ADMIN_CLICK` is added as a new enum value in `payment_service.py` (one-line addition) for admin-approval auditing. If reuse of an existing kind is preferred, this is trivially reversible.
Batch tool accepts 1..20 payments per call. Larger batches should be split by the agent.
The 24h timeout sweep runs at 15-minute intervals inside `AdminChatCog` (not `PaymentWorkerCog`) because `AdminChatCog` owns intent lifecycle.
`safe_delete_messages` is called on admin approval to delete the thread's wallet-prompt + receipt-confirmation-prompt messages (tracked via `prompt_message_id`).
`upsert_wallet_for_user` requires an explicit admin check at the executor level in addition to the identity routing gate (defense-in-depth).

Existing `_start_admin_payment_flow` auto-confirm behavior for test payments stays unchanged — the test still auto-confirms on-chain; only the post-test handling (`handle_payment_result` test-success branch) is rewritten to enter `awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation` instead of immediately creating the real payment.
Query tools (`query_payment_state`, `query_wallet_state`, `list_recent_payments`) apply existing `_redact_payment_row` / `_redact_wallet_row` helpers — no new redaction logic.
